{{Cite web |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/tariq-sims/summary.html |title=Tariq Sims – Career Stats & Summary |website=Rugby League Project |access-date=2018-07-21}} |updated = 26 July [[Korbin Sims]] (brother)
[[Ruan Sims]] (sister)
[[Reagan Campbell-Gillard]] (cousin) }} }} '''Tariq Sims''' (born 9 February 1990) is a [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji international]] retired rugby league footballer who played as a {{rlp|SR|LF}} and {{rlp|PR}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], [[Newcastle Knights]], [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and [[Melbourne Storm]] in the NRL, and at representative level for [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Country]] and [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the [[State of Origin series]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.cowboys.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display&id=3557&player=tariq-sims&team=Cowboys |title=Tariq Sims: Player profile |website=[[North Queensland Cowboys]] |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111013083208/http://www.cowboys.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display&id=3557&player=tariq-sims&team=Cowboys |archive-date=2011-10-13 |access-date=2011-10-30}} ==Early career== [[File:tariqsims.jpg|thumb|right|Sims playing for the Brisbane Broncos' NYC team in 2009]] Sims was born in [[Gerringong, New South Wales]], Australia. He was educated at [[Kiama High School]]. He played junior football in his home town of [[Gerringong, New South Wales]], before moving to [[Queensland]], where he played in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] from 2008 to 2010. He was initially a [[rugby league positions#wings|winger]], but was told by the Broncos to put on weight and turned into a forward.{{Cite news |last=Badel |first=Peter |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/brisbane-broncos-coach-anthony-griffin-gave-tariq-sims-the-advice-that-has-helped-make-him-a-star/story-e6frexnr-1226113472758 |title=Brisbane Broncos coach Anthony Griffin gave Tariq Sims the advice that has helped make him a star |date=2011-08-12 |work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)]] |access-date=2011-10-30 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240528084924/https://www.webcitation.org/71AaJee3I?url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/brisbane-broncos-coach-anthony-griffin-gave-tariq-sims-the-advice-that-has-helped-make-him-a-star/news-story/ddf28848edb0c8c38e02507d7759695d%3Fnk=6852fec82f6f8734d32be43ed244d63b-1532503629 |archive-date=2024-05-28 |publisher=News Corp}} His brothers, [[Korbin Sims|Korbin]] and [[Ashton Sims|Ashton]], both played with him at the Broncos at the same time; Ashton was a member of the first-grade squad, while Korbin played NYC with him. Tariq was the 2010 Toyota Cup season's player of the year, but he left the Broncos without playing a first-grade match. He and Ashton joined the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] for the 2011 season. Upon leaving the Broncos, he praised the club's youth system for his development as a player, saying that he was leaving only to get the opportunity to play first-grade football.{{Cite news |last=McDonald |first=Margie |url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/news/sport/tariq-sims-tells-darren-lockyer-to-watch-out/story-e6frg7mf-1226113361833 |title=Tariq Sims tells Darren Lockyer to watch out |date=2011-08-12 |work=[[The Australian]] |access-date=2011-10-30 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240528085004/https://www.webcitation.org/71AayVfpe?url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/tariq-sims-tells-darren-lockyer-to-watch-out/news-story/5e2a701bf0867d86a83a67ba373dba9f%3Fnk=a22276f670a4e1b297d80f04393992d0-1532504232 |archive-date=2024-05-28 |publisher=News Corp}} ==Playing career== ===2011–2014: North Queensland Cowboys=== In round 2 of the [[2011 NRL season]], Sims made his NRL debut for North Queensland against the [[Newcastle Knights]] off the interchange bench in the Cowboys 34–22 loss at [[1300SMILES Stadium]].{{Cite news |url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/tariq-sims-set-for-nrl-debut-for-cowboys-against-newcastle/news-story/95c734305bcd8fd48d419f9e8f385e09 |title=Tariq Sims set for NRL debut for Cowboys against Newcastle |date=2011-03-15 |work=The Courier-Mail |access-date=2015-10-01 |publisher=News Corp |agency=Australian Associated Press}}{{Cite news |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/newcastle-knights-beat-north-queensland-cowboys-34-22-in-nrl-round-two/news-story/d9703e550b4527f5cd1a730cb043fb3a |title=Newcastle Knights beat North Queensland Cowboys 34–22 in NRL Round two |date=2011-03-20 |work=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=2015-10-01 }} In Sims' next match in round 3 against the [[Melbourne Storm]], he scored his first NRL career try in North Queensland's 34–6 win at 1300SMILES Stadium.{{Cite web |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/north-queensland-cowboys-playing-melbourne-storm-at-dairy-farmers-stadium-in-wet-conditions/story-fn2mcuj6-1226029626669 |title=Matt Bowen, Johnathan Thurston play starring roles as North Queensland Cowboys beat Melbourne Storm |date=2011-03-29 |website=Fox Sports |access-date=2015-10-01}} In round 13 against the [[Canberra Raiders]] at [[Canberra Stadium]], Sims scored a double in the match. One of the tries was the longest try scored by a North Queensland forward by running 45m to score in the Cowboys comeback 40–24 win after trailing 22–0 after 21 minutes of the match.{{Cite news |last=Saddler |first=Matt |url=http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=8257624 |title=Cowboys in huge comeback win over Raiders |date=2011-06-05 |work=Wide World of Sports |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150930152430/http://wwos.ninemsn.com.au/article.aspx?id=8257624 |archive-date=2015-09-30 |publisher=nineMSN |agency=Australian Associated Press}} In Round 25 against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], Sims' season ended when he broke his leg in the Cowboys 28–20 win.{{Cite news |last=Badel |first=Peter |url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/tariq-sims-breaks-leg-in-north-queensland-cowboys-win-over-cronulla-sharks/story-e6frep5x-1226123566348 |title=Cowboys' Tariq Sims out for NRL season after breaking leg in win over Sharks |date=2011-08-28 |work=[[The Sunday Mail (Brisbane)]] |access-date=2011-10-30 }} Despite his curtailed season, Sims was a finalist in the best rookie and best second-rower categories of the Dally M Awards.{{Cite news |last=Crawley |first=Paul |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/north-queenslands-tariq-sims-favourite-to-take-out-dally-m-rookie-of-the-year-title/news-story/8b176a1d750682511801cbf65533e833 |title=North Queensland's Tariq Sims favourite to take out Dally M rookie of the year title |date=2011-07-27 |work=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=2015-10-01 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180726201641/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/north-queenslands-tariq-sims-favourite-to-take-out-dally-m-rookie-of-the-year-title/news-story/8b176a1d750682511801cbf65533e833 |archive-date=2018-07-26 }} During the year, Sims was invited to join the New South Wales State of Origin team during training. Sims finished his stellar debut year in the NRL with him playing in 20 matches and scoring 5 tries. On 8 September 2011, Sims re-signed with North Queensland on a four-year contract to the end of the 2015 season.{{Cite news |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/rookie-forward-tariq-sims-sticking-with-north-queensland-cowboys-until-2015-after-signing-three-year-extension/news-story/887b8c9ba30791eebb1d11332912c6fc |title=Rookie forward Tariq Sims sticking with North Queensland Cowboys until 2015 after signing three-year extension |date=2011-09-07 |work=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=2015-10-01 |via=Townsville Bulletin}} On 2 November 2011, Sims was shortlisted for the Rugby League International Federation's Second Rower of the Year award.{{Cite web |url=http://www.rlif.com/rugby-league-international-news/article/657/2011-international-player-of-the |title=2011 International Player of the Year shortlists announced |date=2011-11-01 |website=Rugby League International Federation |publisher=Rugby League Central |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160309074921/http://www.rlif.com/rugby-league-international-news/article/657/2011-international-player-of-the |archive-date=2016-03-09 |access-date=2015-10-01}} After breaking his leg early in 2012, Sims returned in Round 5 to play in the Cowboys 22–6 victory over the [[Canberra Raiders]] at Canberra Stadium. Sims came off the bench in the second half and put in a memorable performance, performing a big hit on [[Tom Learoyd-Lahrs]] in his first few minutes on the field.{{Cite news |last=Nicolussi |first=Christian |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/raiders-v-cowboys-lowdown/news-story/c7c9edc6860a9f4c929a988755316396 |title=North Queensland Cowboys beat Canberra Raiders 22–6 in NRL round five clash |date=2012-04-03 |work=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=2015-10-01 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180726201648/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/raiders-v-cowboys-lowdown/news-story/c7c9edc6860a9f4c929a988755316396 |archive-date=2018-07-26 }} Sims soon returned to the Cowboys starting line-up. On 22 April 2012, Sims made his representative debut for [[New South Wales Country rugby league team|NSW Country Origin]] against [[New South Wales City rugby league team|NSW City Origin]] off the interchange bench and scored a double in the 24–22 loss at [[Mudgee, New South Wales|Mudgee]].{{Cite news |last=McCullough |first=Ian |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/steve-turner-tariq-sims-and-blake-ferguson-all-score-doubles-as-city-beat-country-in-mudgee/story-e6frf3ou-1226335547287 |title=Steve Turner, Tariq Sims and Blake Ferguson All Score Doubles as City Beat Country in Mudgee |date=2012-04-22 |work=Fox Sports |access-date=2015-10-01 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20150404110317/http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/steve-turner-tariq-sims-and-blake-ferguson-all-score-doubles-as-city-beat-country-in-mudgee/story-e6frf3ou-1226335547287 |archive-date=2015-04-04 |agency=Australian Associated Press}} Just 7 matches into his comeback from a broken leg, playing against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Sims attempted to save a try but was involved in a freak accident with Penrith back rower [[Cameron Ciraldo]] which ended with Sims breaking his leg for a second time in the space of a year and in exactly the same spot as the previous break. The Cowboys went on to win the match 30–28, with Tariq's brother Ashton scoring the match-winning try in the dying minutes of the game.{{Cite news |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/the-lowdown-cowboys-v-panthers/news-story/dde2c797e56d1d1b563aa1ab6884abf4 |title=Tariq Sims breaks leg as North Queensland beat Penrith Panthers in NRL thriller |date=2012-05-19 |work=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=2015-10-01 }} Sims had a successful surgery which involved a steel rod being inserted into his leg to help with the healing process and to stop another break. He returned to full contact training in the week of the Cowboys' semi-final loss to the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]]. Sims played in 7 matches and scored 1 try in [[2012 NRL season|2012]]. On 21 December, Sims was taken to hospital after suffering from dehydration at North Queensland's off season army-style boot camp at [[Lavarack Barracks]].{{Cite news |last=Stewart |first=Antony |url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/cowboy-big-man-tariq-sims-in-hospital-following-pre-season-boot-camp-drama/news-story/01292075beda205dc452ae42dc312346 |title=Cowboy big man Tariq Sims in hospital following pre-season boot camp drama |date=2012-12-20 |work=Townsville Bulletin |access-date=2015-10-01 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180726080851/https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/cowboy-big-man-tariq-sims-in-hospital-following-pre-season-boot-camp-drama/news-story/01292075beda205dc452ae42dc312346 |archive-date=2018-07-26 |publisher=News Corp}} In 2013, Sims made his second return from injury in a 28–24 trial victory over his former club [[Brisbane Broncos]] on 4 February. He started the match in the second row and had three stints in the game. Impressing coaching staff and full of confidence.{{cite web |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/returning-tariq-sims-inspires-north-queensland-cowboys-to-28-24-nrl-trial-win-over-brisbane-broncos/news-story/9288c296e3196d31fabacf540bbe3ae3 |title=Returning Tariq Sims inspires North Queensland Cowboys to 28-24 NRL trial win over Brisbane Broncos |work=Fox Sports |date=9 February 2013 |access-date=26 April 2023}} On 21 April, Sims played for [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|NSW Country Origin]] against [[New South Wales rugby league team|NSW City Origin]] off the interchange bench in the 18–12 win at [[Coffs Harbour, New South Wales|Coffs Harbour]].{{Cite news |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/country-overcome-city-in-close-clash/story-e6frf3ou-1226625366206 |title=Akuila Uate puts his hand up for State of Origin after starring for Country in win over City |last=McCullogh |first=Ian |date=2013-04-21 |work=Fox Sports |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20150219080921/http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/country-overcome-city-in-close-clash/story-e6frf3ou-1226625366206 |archive-date=2015-02-19 |access-date=2015-10-01 |agency=Australian Associated Press}} Sims finished the [[2013 NRL season|season]] with 4 tries from 23 matches. [[File:Tariq Sims.jpg|thumb|right|Sims playing for Fiji in 2013]] Qualifying through his mother, Sims was selected in the [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] alongside his brothers, Ashton and Korbin.{{Cite news |last=Crawley |first=Paul |url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/burgess-and-sims-brothers-set-to-clash-in-rl-world-cup-family-affair/news-story/6cc85fd5c0fd2cb06d3447aff01d005d |title=Burgess and Sims brothers set to clash in RL World Cup family affair |date=2013-11-05 |work=The Courier-Mail |access-date=2015-10-01 |publisher=News Corp}} He made his international debut for Fiji against [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] at second-row and scored a try in the 32–14 win at [[Spotland Stadium]].{{Cite news |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24694176 |title=Rugby League World Cup 2013: Fiji 32–14 Ireland |date=2013-10-28 |work=BBC Sport |access-date=2015-10-01 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180722015759/https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/24694176 |archive-date=2018-07-22 |publisher=British Broadcasting Corporation}} Sims played in 5 matches and scored 1 try in the tournament. On 4 May 2014, Sims played prop for NSW Country Origin in the 26–26 draw at [[Dubbo, New South Wales|Dubbo]].{{Cite news |last=Toohey |first=Barry |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hulking-centre-jamal-idris-stars-in-thrilling-citycountry-draw-in-dubbo/news-story/b70ae3a917003289431f9c52dc0dee19 |title=Hulking centre Jamal Idris stars in thrilling City-Country draw in Dubbo |date=2014-05-04 |work=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=2015-10-01 }} On 4 July, Sims signed a two-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2015, where he would join his younger brother Korbin Sims.{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/knights-sign-tariq-sims-on-two-year-deal/tabid/10874/newsid/79942/default.aspx |title=Knights sign Tariq Sims on two-year deal |date=2014-07-04 |website=NRL.com |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180726225122/https://www.nrl.com/news/2014/07/04/knights-sign-tariq-sims-on-two-year-deal/ |archive-date=2018-07-26 |access-date=2015-10-01}} On 16 September 2014, Sims missed the rest of the NRL finals series after making a guilty plea for a grade three shoulder charge offence for a second-half incident with Brisbane Broncos fullback [[Justin Hodges]] in North Queensland's 32–20 victory in Week 1 of the elimination finals at 1300SMILES Stadium.{{Cite news |last=Badel |first=Peter |url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/dazed-brisbane-skipper-justin-hodges-wouldnt-shake-tariq-sims-hand-at-fulltime/news-story/569933ec60d1cd94beb0e13080ac3ef2?sv=cfd5e35f7080b637ab7fbe5f740da13f |title=Dazed Brisbane skipper Justin Hodges wouldn't shake Tariq Sims' hand at fulltime |date=2014-09-13 |work=The Courier-Mail |access-date=2015-10-01 |publisher=News Corp}} That match was Sims' last for the Cowboys. The Cowboys were later eliminated in Week 2 by the [[Sydney Roosters]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/roosters-survive-miracle-comeback/tabid/10874/newsid/81993/default.aspx |title=Roosters survive miracle comeback |last=Kennedy |first=Chris |date=2014-09-14 |website=NRL.com |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180727005000/https://www.nrl.com/news/2014/09/19/roosters-survive-miracle-comeback/ |archive-date=2018-07-27 |access-date=2015-10-01}} Sims scored 6 tries in 21 matches in [[2014 NRL season|2014]]. ===2015–2016: Newcastle Knights=== In round 5 of the 2015 season, Sims made his club debut for the [[Newcastle Knights]] against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] at second-row in the Knights 13–0 loss at [[Hunter Stadium]].{{Cite news |last=Toohey |first=Barry |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/knights/newcastle-knights-to-unleash-tariq-sims-robbie-rochow-set-for-back-surgery/news-story/075d4a3dca0a9397d85724c6b145b191 |title=Newcastle Knights to unleash Tariq Sims, Robbie Rochow set for back surgery |date=2015-03-29 |work=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=2015-10-01 }}{{Cite news |last=Toohey |first=Barry |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/knights/nrl-2015-beau-scott-injured-as-st-george-illawarra-dragons-keep-newcastle-knights-scoreless/news-story/56d73bcd394dcc775df4675458af56da |title=NRL 2015: Beau Scott injured as St George Illawarra Dragons keep Newcastle Knights scoreless |date=2015-04-03 |work=The Daily Telegraph |access-date=2015-10-01 }} On 3 May, Sims played for NSW Country off the interchange bench and scored a try in the 34–22 win at [[Wagga Wagga, New South Wales|Wagga Wagga]].{{Cite news |last=Dillon |first=Robert |url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/3053574/knights-shine-as-country-carve-up-city-rivals-in-origin-audition/ |title=Knights shine as Country carve up City rivals in Origin audition |date=2015-05-03 |work=Newcastle Herald |access-date=2015-10-01 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150929234752/https://www.theherald.com.au/story/3053574/knights-shine-as-country-carve-up-city-rivals-in-origin-audition/ |archive-date=2015-09-29 |publisher=Fairfax Media |agency=Australian Associated Press}} Sims scored his first club try for the Knights in the 30–10 loss to the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], [[Brookvale Oval]] in Round 9, 2016.{{Cite news |last=Keeble |first=Brett |url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/3068353/manly-hand-knights-fifth-straight-loss/ |title=Manly hand Knights fifth straight loss |date=2015-05-10 |work=Newcastle Herald |access-date=2015-10-01 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150810064837/https://www.theherald.com.au/story/3068353/manly-hand-knights-fifth-straight-loss/ |archive-date=2015-08-10 |publisher=Fairfax Media}} On 12 June, Sims was called into the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Blues]] squad as cover, being named as 19th man for Game 2 of the [[2015 State of Origin series]] after Dragons forward [[Tyson Frizell]] was ruled out from injury. Brother [[Korbin Sims|Korbin]] was named as 20th man for [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] two days beforehand.{{Cite news |last1=Ritchie |first1=Dean |url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-2015-tariq-sims-called-into-blues-camp-could-oppose-maroons-brother-korbin-sims/news-story/6d2cccc68d0e65bc38c23f074b63df75 |title=State of Origin 2015: Tariq Sims called into Blues camp, could oppose Maroons brother Korbin Sims |date=2015-06-12 |work=The Courier-Mail |access-date=2015-10-01 |publisher=News Corp |last2=Macsmith |first2=James |via=Daily Telegraph}} Sims finished his [[2015 NRL season|first year]] with the Knights with him playing in 19 matches and scoring 4 tries, as the club collected the wooden spoon. On 13 January, Sims was named in the emerging [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Blues]] squad.{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/daley-names-2016-emerging-blues/tabid/10874/newsid/91337/default.aspx |title=Daley names 2016 Emerging Blues |date=2016-01-13 |website=NRL.com |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180727013327/https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/01/13/daley-names-2016-emerging-blues/ |archive-date=2018-07-27 |access-date=2016-01-15}} In February, he co-captained the Knights in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].{{Cite web |url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/ |title=Knights Announce Nines Squad |date=2016-01-28 |website=Rugby League Week |publisher=Bauer Media Australia |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130081458/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/ |archive-date=2016-01-30 |access-date=2016-02-04}}{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/2016-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-official-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/91563/default.aspx |title=2016 Downer NRL Auckland Nines Official Squads |date=2016-02-03 |website=NRL.com |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180113202950/https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/02/03/2016-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-official-squads/ |archive-date=2018-01-13 |access-date=2016-02-04}} On 26 February, he was announced as one of three co-captains for the Knights alongside [[Trent Hodkinson]] and [[Jeremy Smith (rugby league born 1980)|Jeremy Smith]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/content/knights/club/news/2016/02/26/newcastle_knights_an.html |title=Newcastle Knights announce 2016 captains |date=2016-02-26 |website=Newcastle Knights |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304131828/https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/content/knights/club/news/2016/02/26/newcastle_knights_an.html |archive-date=2016-03-04 |access-date=2016-02-26}} On 8 May, for the fifth year in a row, he played for New South Wales Country against New South Wales City.{{Cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/young-city-side-stun-country/tabid/10874/newsid/95694/default.aspx |title=Young City side stun Country |last=Webeck |first=Tony |date=2016-02-28 |website=NRL.com |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180626163701/https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/05/08/young-city-side-stun-country/ |archive-date=2018-06-26 |access-date=2016-05-10}} On 31 May, he signed a 3-year contract with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] starting in 2017.{{Cite web |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/dragons-sign-tariq-sims-22121/ |title=Dragons sign Tariq Sims |date=2016-05-31 |website=Zero Tackle |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160604231000/https://www.zerotackle.com/dragons-sign-tariq-sims-22121/ |archive-date=2016-06-04 |access-date=2016-06-16}} However, after negotiating over the next month, he was released early to the Dragons mid-season.{{Cite web |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/tariq-sims-granted-release-22429/ |title=Tariq Sims granted release |date=2016-06-16 |website=Zero Tackle |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160620144206/https://www.zerotackle.com/tariq-sims-granted-release-22429/ |archive-date=2016-06-20 |access-date=2016-06-16}} He played in 8 matches for the Knights that season before the mid-season switch. ===2016–2022: St George Illawarra Dragons=== In round 15 of the 2016 season, he made his club debut for [[St. George Illawarra]] against the [[Melbourne Storm]], where he played off the interchange bench in the Dragons' 20–10 win at [[Wollongong Showground|WIN Stadium]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.nrl.com/tariqs-tough-transition/tabid/10874/newsid/98226/default.aspx |title=Tariq's tough transition |last=Brady |first=Jack |date=2016-02-28 |website=NRL.com |publisher=National Rugby League |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180727034709/https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/06/19/tariqs-tough-transition/ |archive-date=2018-07-27 |access-date=2016-06-19}} In 2017, Sims was part of the [[St. George Illawarra]] side which finished 9th in the [[2017 NRL season]] despite being in the top 8 for most of the season. A disastrous run of form, culminating in a heart breaking round 26 loss to Canterbury which allowed North Queensland to overtake them into 8th spot, saw them miss out on the finals.{{Cite news |last=Reid |first=Angus |url=http://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/ben-hunt-james-graham-st-george-illawarra-dragons-tariq-sims/1068lx8ctcp5q1geymceiivz8g |title=Tariq Sims can't wait to take field with new Dragons recruits Ben Hunt and James Graham |date=2018-02-15 |work=Sporting News |access-date=2018-07-27 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180612144906/http://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/ben-hunt-james-graham-st-george-illawarra-dragons-tariq-sims/1068lx8ctcp5q1geymceiivz8g |archive-date=2018-06-12}} In 2018, Sims was selected as part of The NSW Blues squad for the [[2018 State of Origin series]].{{Cite news |last=Blair |first=Alex |url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-nsw-state-of-origin-team-announcement/news-story/894f8bec422b09d15e159f6304203d6f |title=Live: NSW State of Origin team announcement |date=2018-05-29 |work=News.com.au |access-date=2018-07-27 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180528070109/https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-nsw-state-of-origin-team-announcement/news-story/894f8bec422b09d15e159f6304203d6f |archive-date=2018-05-28 |publisher=News Corp |language=en}} Sims did not feature in the first two games of the series for The Blues but was called into the side for the [[dead rubber]] game 3 match in [[Brisbane]].{{Cite news |last=Chammas |first=Michael |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/07/12/tariq-sims-regrets-brain-explosion-stat-of-origin-penalty-on-ben-hunt/ |title=Tariq Sims regrets brain explosion Stat of Origin penalty on Ben Hunt |date=2018-07-12 |work=National Rugby League |access-date=2018-07-27 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180712083235/https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/07/12/tariq-sims-regrets-brain-explosion-stat-of-origin-penalty-on-ben-hunt/ |archive-date=2018-07-12 |language=en-AU}} Sims was part of the St George Illawarra side which qualified for the finals in 2018. In week one of the finals, St George Illawarra faced a highly fancied Brisbane side at [[Suncorp Stadium]] with not many experts predicting an upset. St George Illawarra ended up surprising many as they won the match 48–18 with Sims scoring 3 tries. The following week, Sims was taken from the field with a knee injury as St George Illawarra were eliminated from the finals series losing 13–12 to South Sydney.{{Cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/09/player-ratings-brisbane-broncos-v-st-george-illawarra-dragons/ |title=Player ratings: Broncos v Dragons |last1=Hazlewood |first1=Scott |last2=Lenehan |first2=Martin |date=2018-09-09 |website=NRL.com |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180919171851/https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/09/player-ratings-brisbane-broncos-v-st-george-illawarra-dragons/ |archive-date=2018-09-19 |access-date=2018-09-22}}{{Cite web |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/live-nrl-south-sydney-rabbitohs-v-st-george-illawarra-dragons-live-score-updates-news-blog/news-story/434579d0fdbe938577ce91970d402046 |title=Adam Reynolds slots three field goals to carry Rabbitohs to victory over Dragons |last=Whaley |first=Pamela |date=2018-09-15 |website=Fox Sports Australia |language=en-AU |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180916140508/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/live-nrl-south-sydney-rabbitohs-v-st-george-illawarra-dragons-live-score-updates-news-blog/news-story/434579d0fdbe938577ce91970d402046 |archive-date=2018-09-16 |access-date=2018-09-22}} Sims was selected for New South Wales in Game 2 of the [[2019 State of Origin series]] which New South Wales won 38–6 at the new [[Perth Stadium]]. In round 21 of the [[2019 NRL season]], Sims scored 2 tries as St George Illawarra defeated the Gold Coast 40–28 at [[Kogarah Oval]].{{Cite news |last=Pengilly |first=Adam |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/trbojevic-and-addo-carr-lead-try-spree-as-blues-level-series-in-perth-20190623-p520gn.html/ |title=Trbojevic and Addo-Carr lead origin try spree |date=2019-06-23 |work=Sydney Morning Herald |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190623213950/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/trbojevic-and-addo-carr-lead-try-spree-as-blues-level-series-in-perth-20190623-p520gn.html |archive-date=2019-06-23 |url-access=limited}}{{Cite web |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-gold-coast-titans-st-george-illawarra-dragons-coach-defence/5xhsb3rxkpuc1wnerzm4lpu98/ |title=Dragons vs Titans: Gold Coast Titans defence slammed as 'too easy for first grade' |last=McKirdy |first=Lachlan |date=2019-08-10 |website=Sporting News |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190812093454/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-gold-coast-titans-st-george-illawarra-dragons-coach-defence/5xhsb3rxkpuc1wnerzm4lpu98/ |archive-date=2019-08-12}} On 12 August, Sims was ruled out for the rest of the season after having groin surgery. Sims had been battling the groin injury for most of the year but decided to undergo surgery as St George Illawarra had been ruled out of finals contention.{{Cite news |last=Encarnacion |first=Matt |url=https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/dragons-tariq-sims-out-for-nrl-season-c-395935/ |title=Dragons' Tariq Sims out for NRL season |date=2019-08-12 |work=7 News |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190820075126/https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/dragons-tariq-sims-out-for-nrl-season-c-395935 |archive-date=2019-08-20 |agency=Australian Associated Press}} He made a total of 9 appearances for St. George Illawarra in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the side finished a disappointing 13th on the table.{{Cite news |last=O'Loughlin |first=Liam |date=2020-10-26 |title=NRL 2020 Season Review: How will your side fare next year? |work=Sporting News |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-season-review-rugby-league-dragons-broncos-titans-tigers-knights-sharks-roosters-eels-rabbitohs-raiders-panthers-storm/k79lmelm2xgj1ffsf8xh7phob/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201026175328/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-season-review-rugby-league-dragons-broncos-titans-tigers-knights-sharks-roosters-eels-rabbitohs-raiders-panthers-storm/k79lmelm2xgj1ffsf8xh7phob/ |archive-date=2020-10-26}} In round 4 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Sims put in a man of the match performance scoring two tries in his side's 22–13 victory over Newcastle.{{Cite news |last=Bruce |first=Jasper |date=2021-04-04 |title=Tariq Sims' classy gesture in heated NRL clash |work=News.com.au |url=https://news.com.au/sport/nrl/tariq-sims-classy-gesture-in-heated-nrl-clash/news-story/bd503a1aecc29017f25d1dc1c2fd72d2/ |access-date=2021-04-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210531222705/https://amp.news.com.au/sport/nrl/tariq-sims-classy-gesture-in-heated-nrl-clash/news-story/bd503a1aecc29017f25d1dc1c2fd72d2/ |archive-date=2021-05-31}} On 30 May, Sims was selected for game one of the [[2021 State of Origin series]]. He played in all three games as New South Wales won the series 2–1.{{Cite web |last=Walsh |first=Dan |date=2021-05-30 |title=NSW Origin team: Luai gets nod for Freddie's fresh-faced Blues |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/05/30/nsw-blues-origin-i-team/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210531223824/https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/05/30/nsw-blues-origin-i-team/ |archive-date=2021-05-31 |access-date=2021-05-31 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}} Sims played a total of 21 matches for [[St. George Illawarra]] in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club finished 11th on the table and missed out on the finals.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-st-george-illawarra-dragons-vs-north-queensland-cowboys-live-stream-blog-score-updates-supercoach/news-story/bb4205d15445f75b33404ef10c54a3b6/|title=Luckless Cowboy ends 14-game losing streak; Dragons find their new No.6: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=28 August 2021}} On 22 April, it was announced Sims would join [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]] in 2023 on a one-year deal. On 29 May, Sims was selected by New South Wales to play in game one of the [[2022 State of Origin series]].{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/05/29/hynes-staggs-matterson-headline-blues-squad-for-origin-opener/|title=Wighton starts at centre as Blues confirm lineup for Game One|website=www.nrl.com|date=30 May 2022 }} On 19 June, Sims was not selected for the second game of the series after New South Wales lost the opening match 16–10.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/state-of-origin-2022-winners-and-losers-matt-burton-siosifa-talakai-jake-trbojevic-angus-crichton-kotoni-staggs/news-story/a6f75b00a20512f7b46a2c113ebbd737/|title=13-game veteran recalled, Eels pair pay the price: Blues Origin II Winners and Losers|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=20 June 2022 }} In round 17 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Sims was sin binned twice in 25 minutes during the clubs 32–18 loss against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-tariq-sims-sin-bin-two-sin-bins-high-tackle-dangerous-contact-st-george-illawarra-dragons-brisbane-broncos-adam-reynolds-injury-news/news-story/ee50c4405c06ab4155ba0cc439d4814c/|title='A chair with his name on it': Sims binned TWICE in 25 minutes for two ugly tackles|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=10 July 2022 }} In round 21, Sims was sent to the sin bin for a dangerous high tackle where he knocked out Cronulla's [[Connor Tracey]] during St. George Illawarra's 24–18 loss. Sims was later suspended for four matches over the tackle which ended his playing time at St. George Illawarra.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-tariq-sims-tackle-dragons-connor-tracey-sharks-ashley-klein-sin-bin/news-story/c9b80ea65e4f32d18210a01e821b48d3/|title=NRL ref Ashley Klein blasted as 'gutless' for failing to send off Dragons enforcer|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=7 August 2022 }} ===Melbourne Storm=== Sims played 15 games for Melbourne in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished third on the table. Sims played in Melbourne's preliminary final loss against Penrith.{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-melbourne-storm-season-review/af9dab1e4f900cda437dc53f#:~:text=The%20expectations%20for%20the%20Storm,clubs%2C%20but%20not%20the%20Storm./|title=NRL 2023: Melbourne Storm season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}} ===Catalans Dragons=== On 28 August 2023, Sims signed a two-year deal to join [[Super League]] side the [[Catalans Dragons]].{{cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/3750/sims-signs-for-dragons#:~:text=Catalans%20Dragons%20have%20announced%20the,old%20with%20North%20Queensland%20Cowboys./|title=Sims signs for Dragons|website=www.superleague.co.uk}} Sims made his club debut for Catalans in round 1 of the 2024 Super League season against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]. The following week, he scored two tries for Catalans in their 34-0 victory over the newly promoted [[London Broncos]] side.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337683|title=Super League: London Broncos 0-34 Catalans Dragons - visitors cruise to win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} On 22 September 2025, Sims officially confirmed his retirement from rugby league.{{Cite web |last=Issa |first=Isaac |date=2025-09-22 |title=Former NSW Blues star calls time on 14-year career |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/former-nsw-blues-star-calls-time-on-14-year-career-227086/ |access-date=2025-09-22 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}} == Statistics == {{cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/players/nrl-premiership/storm/tariq-sims/ |title= Tariq Sims |publisher=NRL |access-date=29 June 2023}} {| class="wikitable" |+ !Year !Team !Games !Tries !Pts |- |2011 | rowspan="4" |{{leagueicon|North Queensland|16}} [[North Queensland Cowboys]] | align=right|20 | align=right|5 | align=right|20 |- |2012 | align=right|7 | align=right|1 | align=right|4 |- |2013 | align=right|23 | align=right|4 | align=right|16 |- |2014 | align=right|21 | align=right|6 | align=right|14 |- |2015 |{{leagueicon|Newcastle|16}} [[Newcastle Knights]] | align=right|19 | align=right|4 | align=right|16 |- |2016 | rowspan="7" |{{leagueicon|St. george|16}} [[St George-Illawarra Dragons]] | align=right|16 | align=right|2 | align=right|8 |- |2017 | align=right|24 | align=right|3 | align=right|12 |- |2018 | align=right|25 | align=right|10 | align=right|40 |- |2019 | align=right|19 | align=right|3 | align=right|12 |- |2020 | align=right|9 | align=right| | align=right| |- |2021 | align=right|21 | align=right|4 | align=right|16 |- |2022 | align=right|17 | align=right|1 | align=right|4 |- |2023 |{{leagueicon|Melbourne|16}} [[Melbourne Storm]] | align=right|13 | align=right|1 | align=right|4 |- |2024 | rowspan="2" |{{leagueicon|catalans|16}} [[Catalans Dragons]] | align=right|22 | align=right|3 | align=right|12 |- |2025 |13 |2 |8 |- | |Totals | align="right" |268 | align="right" |49 | align="right" |196 |} ==Personal life== Sims is married to Ashleigh Sims who is a professional boxer. Sims is the older brother of [[Korbin Sims]], who in 2019 joined him at the St George Illawarra club, and the younger brother of [[Ashton Sims]],{{Cite news |last=Stewart |first=Antony |url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/ashton-tariq-and-korbin-sims-will-play-for-fiji-in-this-years-world-cup/news-story/aa2fbd9ec920c9639456cbfa7d5a1f9d |title=Ashton, Tariq and Korbin Sims will play for Fiji in this year's World Cup |work=Courier Mail |access-date=31 May 2015 |publisher=News Corp}} who plays for the [[Toronto Wolfpack]]. His elder sister [[Ruan Sims]] currently plays for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[NRL Women's Premiership]], while his other sister, Canecia (CJ) has previously played [[American football]] (gridiron) for the [[Gold Coast Stingrays]],{{Cite magazine |last=Cadigan |first=Neil |date=March 2016 |title=Sim-ply the best - Ruan Sims |url=https://menofleague.com/sim-ply-best-ruan-sims/ |magazine=Men of League Foundation Magazine |issue=62 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210608003634/https://menofleague.com/sim-ply-best-ruan-sims/ |archive-date=2021-06-08 |access-date=2021-06-08}} but in 2019 signed with the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]] rugby league club.{{Cite news |date=2019-01-24 |title=Seagulls turn to Sims family for marquee signing |work=[[Gold Coast Bulletin]] |url=https://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/local-sport/tweed-heads-seagulls-sign-cj-sims-for-their-inaugural-womens-rugby-league-team/news-story/89a3200dc06e7f48e67000b311909e89 |url-access=subscription |access-date=2021-06-08}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://www.dragons.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/st-george-illawarra-dragons/tariq-sims/ St.
